name: vscode-starlims
description: Unofficial dictionary explorer (and more) for STARLIMS.

author.name: Christoph Döllinger, Marius Popovici, Jan Bouecke

commands.title: View checked out items of all users
Export all checked out items to SDP

(view == STARLIMSMainTree || view == STARLIMSCheckedOutTree) && viewItem != SERVERLOG && viewItem != CATEGORY && viewItem != TABLE && viewItem != ENT_TABLES_DATABASE && viewItem != ENT_TABLES_DICTIONARY && !(viewItem =~ /cat/i)
view == STARLIMSTicketsTree && (viewItem == ticket || viewItem == activeTicket) && viewItem != lockedTicket
view == STARLIMSTicketsTree && (viewItem == activeTicket || viewItem == ticketInProgressByCurrentUser)

view == STARLIMSTicketsTree && (viewItem == ticket || viewItem == activeTicket)

STARLIMS.url.description: STARLIMS Installation URL. For example: https://my.starlims.server.com/STARLIMS/

STARLIMS.userPassword.description: STARLIMS User Password. This value is not stored in the settings file.

STARLIMS.rootPath.description: Root path for downloaded STARLIMS files. For example: C:\STARLIMS\. SLVSCODE will be appended to this path.

STARLIMS.browser.description: Browser to use for debugging STARLIMS forms. Possible values: chrome, msedge

STARLIMS.urlSuffix.description: The web service request url suffix. Use lims2 with older versions of the STARLIMS Technology Platform which don't recognize the HTTPServices web.config setting key

STARLIMS.defaultFormLanguage.description: Default language to use when checking out STARLIMS form items. Defaults to GER.

STARLIMS.mcp.enabled.description: Enable the local STARLIMS MCP endpoint on the built-in loopback HTTP server. Only browse, search, code retrieval, and checkout are exposed. Check-in, delete, and other destructive operations remain unavailable.

STARLIMS.mcp.maxItems.description: Maximum number of STARLIMS items returned by a single MCP browse or search request.

STARLIMS.mcp.port.description: Loopback TCP port used by the STARLIMS MCP endpoint. This is separate from the form callback server, which uses a free port in the range 3003-3099.

STARLIMS.mcp.maxCodeCharacters.description: Maximum number of code characters returned by a single STARLIMS MCP read request.

STARLIMS.opencode.proxy.enabled.description: Enable an OpenAI-compatible proxy that forwards chat completion requests to an opencode instance.

STARLIMS.opencode.proxy.host.description: Host address the opencode proxy binds to. Use 127.0.0.1 for local-only access.

STARLIMS.opencode.proxy.port.description: TCP port used by the opencode proxy endpoint.

STARLIMS.opencode.proxy.targetUrl.description: Base URL of the OpenCode server that the proxy forwards requests to. The documented default is http://localhost:4096.

STARLIMS.opencode.command.description: Command or absolute executable path used by the Solve with OpenCode ticket action. Use npx.cmd together with STARLIMS.opencode.commandArgs if you launch OpenCode through npx on Windows.

STARLIMS.opencode.commandArgs.description: Additional arguments passed before the ticket-specific OpenCode flags, for example ["opencode"] when STARLIMS.opencode.command is set to npx.cmd.

STARLIMS.opencode.openOnStartup.description: Automatically open OpenCode in a new window when VS Code starts. Uses STARLIMS.opencode.command, STARLIMS.opencode.commandArgs, and STARLIMS.opencode.workingDirectory.

STARLIMS.opencode.planModel.description: Initial OpenCode model used by the Solve with OpenCode ticket action when it launches plan mode.

STARLIMS.opencode.buildModel.description: Preferred follow-up model shown to the user after the OpenCode plan phase. This extension does not switch to it automatically.

STARLIMS.opencode.workingDirectory.description: Optional working directory for Solve with OpenCode. Leave empty to prefer the SLVSCODE workspace root. Relative paths resolve from that same root.

urlSuffix.description: The web service request url suffix

STARLIMS.git.enabled.description: Enable git integration for STARLIMS check-in operations

STARLIMS.git.autoPush.description: Automatically push commits to remote repository after check-in

STARLIMS.git.remoteUrl.description: Git remote repository URL (optional, can be configured separately)

enumDescriptions: Use a fast local fallback message such as 'Update frmTest [Code Behind]' without calling Copilot.
Use Copilot to generate the message, with a short timeout fallback to the fast local message.
STARLIMS.git.commitMessageGenerator.description: How Copilot generates check-in and Git commit messages.

STARLIMS.git.copilotCommitMessageModel.description: Optional exact Copilot chat model name to use for generated check-in and Git commit messages, for example 'Raptor Mini (Preview)'. Leave empty to use the default available Copilot model.

STARLIMS.git.copilotCommitMessageSystemP.default: You write STARLIMS commit messages. Describe the changes in detail. Mention the main item or items based on the diffs. Use bullets where applicable.
STARLIMS.git.copilotCommitMessageSystemP.markdownDescription: Top-level instruction text sent to Copilot before the STARLIMS item context when generating check-in and Git commit messages.

enumDescriptions: Keep messages compact and focused on the item name and change details.
Include balanced context such as item type or change details when helpful.
Prefer richer descriptions that include item name and the change details.
STARLIMS.git.commitMessageDetailLevel.description: How much detail to include in generated STARLIMS commit messages.

STARLIMS.git.commitMessageMaxLength.description: Maximum length for generated STARLIMS commit messages.

STARLIMS.git.commitMessagePrefix.description: Optional prefix added to generated STARLIMS commit messages, for example a ticket or team tag.

STARLIMS.git.includeItemTypeInCommitMess.description: Include the STARLIMS item type in generated commit messages when using standard or detailed modes.

STARLIMS.git.includeLanguageInCommitMess.description: Include the STARLIMS language in generated commit messages when available.

STARLIMS.git.includeFileNameInCommitMess.description: Include the local file name in generated commit messages when available.

STARLIMS.git.copilotCommitMessageTimeout.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for Copilot commit message generation before falling back to the fast local message.

starlimsJsLsp.enabled.description: Enable the STARLIMS JavaScript Language Server for JS IntelliSense in SLVSCODE workspaces.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.build: (rm -rf out || true) && mkdir out && cp package.json out && cd ./src/backend && ./create-packages.sh && cd ../.. && vsce package
scripts.build-windows: if exist out rmdir /s /q out & mkdir out & copy package.json out & cd .\src\backend & powershell.exe .\create-packages.ps1 & cd ..\.. & vsce package
